perturbation.original_text: 3. The Receiving Party agrees (i) to adopt measures to protect the confidentiality, limited use and proprietary nature of the Confidential Information at least as restrictive as those it adopts for its own confidential information of similar importance and in any event, no less than reasonable; (ii) to disclose Confidential Information to only those of its directors, officers, agents, employees, corporate affiliates and third parties retained by the Receiving Party who have a need to know such information in order for the Receiving Party to accomplish the purposes set forth in Paragraph 4 hereof and who are made aware of the confidentiality provisions of this Agreement ( and in the case of a third party, who have signed a binding undertaking of confidentiality and non -use substantially equivalent to this Agreement ), and (iii) to not use Confidential Information for any purpose except as permitted by Paragraph 4. A Receiving Party shall be responsible to the Disclosing Party for any unauthorized u se or disclosure of Confidential Information by any party to whom Receiving Party has disclosed such information.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. The Receiving Party agrees (i) to adopt measures to protect the confidentiality, limited use and proprietary nature of the Confidential Information with minimal effort; (ii) to disclose Confidential Information to all its directors, officers, agents, employees, corporate affiliates and third parties retained by the Receiving Party regardless of the need to know such information in order for the Receiving Party to accomplish the purposes set forth in Paragraph 4 hereof and who are made aware of the confidentiality provisions of this Agreement ( and in the case of a third party, who have signed a binding undertaking of confidentiality and non -use substantially equivalent to this Agreement ), and (iii) to use Confidential Information for any purpose even if its not permitted by Paragraph 4. A Receiving Party shall not be responsible to the Disclosing Party for any unauthorized u se or disclosure of Confidential Information by any party to whom Receiving Party has disclosed such information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text imposes a 'reasonable' standard of care for protecting Confidential Information and limits disclosure to those with a 'need to know'. The modified text eliminates the 'reasonable' standard, removes the 'need to know', allows to use Confidential Information for any purpose and removes the responsibility of the Receiving Party for any unauthorized use or disclosure, effectively nullifying the protection intended. This creates an **in-text contradiction** with the purpose of the agreement itself, which is to protect confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Page 1 of 3 © 2013 Business Innovation Group Pty Ltd Mutual Non -Disclosure Agreement MUTUAL NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT This MUTUAL CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (the “Agreement”) is made and entered into by and among: Business Innovation Group Limited (A.C.N. 165 042 517 ) of The University Centre, Level 8, 210 Clarence Street, Sydney Australia 2000 (“BiG”) and: with offices located at . (“The Company”). Effective as of the ________________ day of _________________ 2013 (the “Effective Date”). WHEREAS, in the course of business discussions, The Company and BiG (each a “Party” and together the “Parties”) shall disclose certain confidential and proprietary information to each other in connection with a proposed business relationship between The Company and BiG (the “Transaction”); and WHEREAS, as a conditi on to such exchange, each Party (each, a “Disclosing Party”) seeks to bind the other Party (each, a “Receiving Party”) to obligations of confidentiality and limited use with respect to the information it discloses. NOW, THEREFORE, IN CONSIDERATION of th e mutual agreements contained herein and the agreement to exchange information as contemplated hereunder, the Parties agree as follows:
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the purpose of the Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) stated in the contradicted text, which is to bind parties to obligations of confidentiality and limited use of disclosed information. The modified text removes the 'reasonable' standard of care, eliminates the 'need to know' restriction for disclosure, allows the use of Confidential Information for any purpose and removes the responsibility of the Receiving Party for any unauthorized use or disclosure. This creates a direct conflict with the NDA's fundamental purpose of protecting confidential information and constitutes a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. The Receiving Party may use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information for the sole purpose of evaluating, negotiating and, in its discr etion, entering into the proposed Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. The Receiving Party may use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever, including but not limited to competing directly with the Disclosing Party, reverse engineering products, or disclosing it to third parties without restriction.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause limits the use of Confidential Information to evaluating and negotiating the proposed transaction. The modified text allows the Receiving Party to use the information for any purpose, including competing with the Disclosing Party. This directly contradicts the foundational premise of a non-disclosure agreement and creates an **in-text contradiction** with the agreement's purpose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. The Parties agree that, in the event of a breach of this Agreement, monetary damages may not be a sufficient remedy and in addition to any other legal remedy , the non -breaching Party shall be entitled to equitable remedies, including injunctive relief. In the event that any Party takes legal action as a result of a breach of this Agreement, the non -breaching Party shall be entitled to recover reasonable legal fees and expenses incurred in connection with such legal action in the event that it prevails in one or more of its claims.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Receiving Party to use Confidential Information for any purpose, including competing with the Disclosing Party. This directly contradicts the potential enforcement of equitable remedies, including injunctive relief, as outlined in the contradicted text (Section 6), since the 'breach of this Agreement' now includes actions that are explicitly permitted by the changed text. The original text limited the use of confidential information which aligned with the enforcement terms of Section 6. The modified text creates an in-text contradiction because the Receiving party could compete with the Disclosing Party, making section 6 un-enforcable.

perturbation.original_text: 5. The Parties shall promptly advise each other in writing if they learn of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information by any of their directors, officers, employees, corporate affiliates, agents or by any third party.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. The Parties are not required to advise each other about the unauthorized use or disclosure of confidential information. They may do so at their own discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement imposes a mandatory duty to report any unauthorized use or disclosure of confidential information. The altered text renders the notification discretionary. This introduction of option contradicts the purpose of ensuring secrecy, and the duty to report. Such **in-text contradiction** undermines the fundamental essence of the agreement to safeguard confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Page 1 of 3 © 2013 Business Innovation Group Pty Ltd Mutual Non -Disclosure Agreement MUTUAL NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT This MUTUAL CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (the “Agreement”) is made and entered into by and among: Business Innovation Group Limited (A.C.N. 165 042 517 ) of The University Centre, Level 8, 210 Clarence Street, Sydney Australia 2000 (“BiG”) and: with offices located at . (“The Company”). Effective as of the ________________ day of _________________ 2013 (the “Effective Date”). WHEREAS, in the course of business discussions, The Company and BiG (each a “Party” and together the “Parties”) shall disclose certain confidential and proprietary information to each other in connection with a proposed business relationship between The Company and BiG (the “Transaction”); and WHEREAS, as a conditi on to such exchange, each Party (each, a “Disclosing Party”) seeks to bind the other Party (each, a “Receiving Party”) to obligations of confidentiality and limited use with respect to the information it discloses. NOW, THEREFORE, IN CONSIDERATION of th e mutual agreements contained herein and the agreement to exchange information as contemplated hereunder, the Parties agree as follows:
perturbation.justification: The original text in clause 5 mandates that parties promptly advise each other of any unauthorized use or disclosure of confidential information. However, the changed text makes this notification discretionary, directly contradicting the original mandatory requirement. The contradicted text is the NDA's foundational assertion that both parties seek to bind each other to obligations of confidentiality and limited use. By removing the duty to report, this agreement's purpose of confidentiality is undermined, creating a strong in-text contradiction as the parties are no longer obligated to take steps to protect shared confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: 7. The provisions of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect beginning as of the Effective Date and shall continue with respect to a Receiving Party for as long as it retains the Confidential Information in its possession and for three (3) years thereafter. A receiving Party shall return or destroy all copies of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to the Disclo sing Party within ten (10) days after the Disclosing Party’s request.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. The provisions of this Agreement shall immediately cease to have any force or effect. The receiving party is not required to return or destroy copies of the disclosing party’s confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: In the original agreement, the confidentiality provisions remain in effect for as long as the receiving party retains the information and for three years after. Further, the receiving party must return or destroy all copies within ten days of request. The modification immediately terminates the entire agreement, and voids all the provisions in it. This **in-text contradiction** voids any legal protection. It is in disagreement with the purpose of the whole document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. The Parties agree that, in the event of a breach of this Agreement, monetary damages may not be a sufficient remedy and in addition to any other legal remedy , the non -breaching Party shall be entitled to equitable remedies, including injunctive relief. In the event that any Party takes legal action as a result of a breach of this Agreement, the non -breaching Party shall be entitled to recover reasonable legal fees and expenses incurred in connection with such legal action in the event that it prevails in one or more of its claims.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement immediately ceases to have any force or effect and that the receiving party is not required to return or destroy confidential information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (Section 6) which outlines remedies for breach of the agreement (including injunctive relief and legal fees) as these remedies rely on the agreement remaining in effect. The changed text eliminates the basis for enforcing the agreement, thus creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. This Agreement expresses the entire agreement between the Parties and supersedes all prior understandings and discussions between the Parties. This Agreement may only be modified, changed or amended by a n agreement in writing exec uted by the Parties. A waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall not be deemed a waiver of any other provision and shall not be binding unless it is in writing and executed by the Party making such waiver.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. This Agreement does not express the entire agreement between the Parties and is subject to prior oral agreements. This Agreement may be unilaterally modified, changed or amended by either party at any time without notice. A verbal waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be deemed a permanent waiver of that provision.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement is an 'entire agreement', meaning that it contains all of the terms agreed to. It cannot be verbally modified and a verbal waiver is not binding. The modified version states that it is subject to prior oral agreements, and can be unilaterally modified by either party, and that verbal waivers are binding. This is a direct **in-text contradiction**, making it unclear what terms apply to the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Page 1 of 3 © 2013 Business Innovation Group Pty Ltd Mutual Non -Disclosure Agreement MUTUAL NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT This MUTUAL CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (the “Agreement”) is made and entered into by and among: Business Innovation Group Limited (A.C.N. 165 042 517 ) of The University Centre, Level 8, 210 Clarence Street, Sydney Australia 2000 (“BiG”) and: with offices located at . (“The Company”). Effective as of the ________________ day of _________________ 2013 (the “Effective Date”). WHEREAS, in the course of business discussions, The Company and BiG (each a “Party” and together the “Parties”) shall disclose certain confidential and proprietary information to each other in connection with a proposed business relationship between The Company and BiG (the “Transaction”); and WHEREAS, as a conditi on to such exchange, each Party (each, a “Disclosing Party”) seeks to bind the other Party (each, a “Receiving Party”) to obligations of confidentiality and limited use with respect to the information it discloses. NOW, THEREFORE, IN CONSIDERATION of th e mutual agreements contained herein and the agreement to exchange information as contemplated hereunder, the Parties agree as follows:
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 9 introduces a direct contradiction. The original text establishes an 'entire agreement' clause, restricting modifications and waivers to written forms. The changed text negates this by stating that the agreement is subject to prior oral agreements, allows unilateral modifications, and deems verbal waivers binding. This directly contradicts the 'WHEREAS' clauses in the Contradicted Text which states that the parties agree to exchange information and abide by the agreement, based on the mutual agreements contained within, which loses its meaning as the Changed Text invalidates the entire agreement clause. This in-text contradiction creates ambiguity and uncertainty about the terms' enforceability.
